quote_of_day.text: Talked to kalej customer care wali for 15+ minutes, zaamn her sweer voice. The way she was answering my intentionally silly questions. Mix of hindi n english, calling me 'aap'. AHHH. She also reacted with red heart on my texts.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste hatred moved into the board's atmosphere today, bringing with it a torrent of vitriolic comments.

Anon's speculation on the physical appearance of a chamar sparked a downpour of replies, with one anon remarking on the perceived preference for white skin among 'pajeets'.

A cold front of discontent swept through the board, fueled by another anon's lamentation over the downfall of Aryan savarna.

The post drew a flurry of responses, with one respondent calling for the removal of a leader described as a 'deep state agent' in a 'tribal shithole'.

Meanwhile, a scattered high-pressure system of observational humor touched down, as one anon drew a comparison between high-rise living and life in a chicken coop.

The post garnered a handful of replies, with another user remarking on the limitations of balcony access during inclement weather.

Elsewhere, brief but intense interactions flared around a woman in a video, described by one anon as 'osam hai', and a doctor mocked on the Pranit More show.

articles.title: Anon Speculates on Caste and Appearance
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread asking how a chamar looks like, then immediately referenced learning that someone they know is a Scheduled Tribe (ST). They wondered aloud how 'White skin loving pajeets' would cope.

Anon responded by suggesting that appearance can be altered with money, citing the example of hormone and facial reconstruction surgeries available at a lower cost in South Korea.

Another anon interjected, pointing out that not all Scheduled Tribes are of the same social standing as chamars, mentioning Rajasthani Meenas and Northeastern people as examples of STs.

A respondent corrected an anon, stating that SC/ST refers specifically to Dalit chamars. However, another anon disagreed, saying that the person in question isn't a Dalit, but rather a member of a Scheduled Tribe.

Anon continued to discuss the nuances of caste classification, with one mentioning that some Dogra Brahmins are classified as ST.

The conversation took a confusing turn when an anon disputed the claim that Dogras are ST, directly calling another anon 'nigga' and stating 'dogra are not ST'.

Anon's original query about appearance remained largely unaddressed as the conversation veered into specifics of caste identity and classification.

articles.title: Anon Laments Aryan Savarna's Downfall
articles.body:
Anon posted a message expressing discontent with the current state of affairs, stating that another Aryan savarna has been sacrificed.

The poster used derogatory language to refer to India, calling it a 'bhangi rapepiblic' and demanded the removal of someone referred to as 'chodi' from the position of Prime Minister, claiming they are a 'deep state agent'.

Another anon responded with a cryptic comment, seemingly referencing a different user named 'fagyush', suggesting that they would have taken drastic action against the thread. This poster also included laughter emojis, indicating a joking tone.

A third anon briefly referenced the original post, addressing someone as 'chodi'. A fourth anon responded with a brief mention of 'fagyush', seemingly in response to the second post.

articles.title: Anon compares high-rise living to chicken coop life
articles.body:
Anon opened the discussion by comparing high-rise living to residing in a chicken coop.

They claimed to feel uncomfortable with the lack of privacy, citing an incident where they were masturbating in their room with open curtains, and someone shone a laser into their room.

Another anon shared their experience of living in a similar high-rise apartment, stating that life was better in their previous low-rise building with more free space to play.

They expressed dissatisfaction with the current living conditions, where sunlight only reaches their apartment for a short interval between 2 and 3 pm.

A respondent suggested that living in a rural village would be a better alternative, but another anon countered that such places are often devoid of activities, leading to boredom. They still considered it a preferable option over soulless concrete jungles.

Anon also discussed the trade-offs of living in different types of cities, with one stating that tier 2 and 3 cities often lack high-paying jobs.

A different anon shared that they live on the 20th floor of a 30-floor condo with a lake view, enjoying complete privacy.

The conversation also touched on socio-economic factors, with one anon stating that only 'white collar dalits' live in high-rise buildings, while 'richfags' reside in bungalows. The discussion remained focused on personal experiences and preferences regarding high-rise living.

articles.title: Anon comments on woman in a video
articles.body:
Anon posted a brief comment on a woman in a video, saying 'Ye kitti osam hai'. Other anons replied with speculation about her background, with one claiming she is an MBBS student at a good college.

Anon also made a remark about her appearance, saying 'Zaamn yaaro look at her eyes'. The conversation took a sexist turn, with an anon making an obscene comment about wanting her and her friend to joke about his dick size.

Another anon called her a 'Misandrist kween'.

articles.title: Doctor mocked on Pranit More show
articles.body: Anon shared details about a doctor, Sejal Rahul Pawar, from KEM hospital in Mumbai. The poster claimed she joked on the Pranit More show about making fun of dead bodies' body parts. Anon called her insensitive, and other responders reacted strongly. One called her a "bitch" and another predicted a violent end. Some responders made personal remarks about the original poster, labeling them an "incel".
